zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir George Bond於1534年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特韋斯特 邦藍時，他的父親William Bond41歲，母親Dionese Bowerman28歲。他於1552年2月5日與Winifred Leigh在英國賀特福爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他於1587年被列為lord mayor of london。他去世於1592年3月4日在英國米德萊可倫敦享年58歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Bond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name for a peasant farmer or husbandman, Middle English bond(e), bounde, occasionally bande ‘bondman, customary tenant, serf’ (Old English bonda, bunda, reinforced by Old Norse bóndi). The Old Norse word was also in use as a personal name (Old Norse Bóndi, Bondi, Bundi, Bonde, borrowed as late Old English Bonda), and this has given rise to other English and Scandinavian surnames alongside those originating as status names, such as the Middle English personal name Bonde. The status of the peasant farmer fluctuated considerably during the Middle Ages; moreover, the underlying ancient Germanic word is of disputed origin and meaning. Among ancient Germanic peoples who settled to an agricultural life, the term came to signify a farmer holding lands from, and bound by loyalty to, a lord; from this developed the sense of a free landholder as opposed to a serf. In England after the Norman Conquest the word sank in status and became associated with the notion of bound servitude. The name can also be a variant of Band .
Swedish: variant of   Bonde  .
In some cases also an American shortened form of Ukrainian Bondarenko and possibly also of some other surname beginning with Bond-.

variants.name: Bundock
meanings: English (Essex): from a Middle English personal name Bondoc, a pet form of Bonde (late Old English Bonda; see Bond 1) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -oc. Compare Bundick .

variants.name: Husband
meanings: English and Scottish (Fife): status name from Middle English housbond(e) ‘male spouse’, ‘householder, male head of a household’, ‘villein or customary tenant who holds land additional to the croft attached to his homestead’, ‘husbandman or farmer’. The term (late Old English hūsbonda, Old Norse húsbóndi) is a compound of hús ‘house’ + bóndi (see Bond ).

variants.name: Bonde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik.
English (Devon): variant of   Bond  .
Danish and Swedish: status name for a farmer, from Old Norse bóndi ‘farmer’ (see also Bond ).

variants.name: Bunde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Gerhard, Hans, Kurt.
North German: status name for a farmer from Middle Low German bunde, bunne, related to Danish, Swedish bonde ‘free peasant’ (see Bond ).

German (Bünde): topographic name from Old High German biunda ‘enclosed fertile pasture’ (modern German Bünde, Beunte), a derivative of biwenden ‘to bend or weave’, with reference to the practice of making fences from woven branches. There are many different local forms and communal implications concerning the use of the land.

variants.name: Bondarenko
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Oleg, Vladimir, Yuriy, Aleksandr, Anatoliy, Anatoly, Gennady, Grigoriy, Igor, Konstantin, Lyubov, Lyudmila.
Ukrainian: patronymic from bondar ‘cooper’, formed with the diminutive suffix -enko. Compare Bond .

variants.name: Bonds
meanings: English: variant of Bond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Bounds
meanings: English: variant of Bond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Bound
meanings: English (southern): variant of   Bond  .

variants.name: Bundy
meanings: variant of Bond , either from Middle English bonde, bounde ‘customary tenant’ where the final -e has developed in local speech to -y, or occasionally from the Old Norse Bondi, where final -i has survived.
alternatively, a variant of   Bandy  .
English:

variants.name: Band
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Isidor, Myer.

English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a maker of hoops and bands, etc., from Middle English band, bond, Middle High German, Middle Low German bant, German Band ‘hoop, metal band, fetter, shackle’, i.e. something used for tying or binding.
English: variant of   Bond  .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Sir George Bond was born in 1534, in West Buckland, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, William Bond, was 41 and his mother, Dionese Bowerman, was 28. He married Winifred Leigh on 5 February 1552, in Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 8 daughters. In 1587, at the age of 53, his occupation is listed as lord mayor of london. He died on 4 March 1592, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 58, and was buried in London, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir George Bond est né en 1534, à West Buckland, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Bond, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Dionese Bowerman, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Winifred Leigh le 5 février 1552, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 8 filles. En 1587, à l’âge de 53 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est lord mayor of london. Il est décédé le 4 mars 1592, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir George Bond 1534 in West Buckland, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Bond 41 und seine Mutter Dionese Bowerman 28. Er hat am 5. Februar 1552 in Hertfordshire, England Winifred Leigh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 8 Töchter. 1587, als er 53 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als lord mayor of london aufgeführt. Er starb am 4. März 1592 im Alter von 58 Jahren in London, Middlesex, England und wurde in London, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir George Bond è nato il nel 1534 nel West Buckland, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William Bond, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Dionese Bowerman, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Winifred Leigh il 5 febbraio 1552, nel Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 8 figlie. Nel 1587, all'età di 53 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come lord mayor of london. Morì il 4 marzo 1592, nel London, Middlesex, England, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a London, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir George Bondが1534年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトウエスト ブックランドで生まれた時，父William Bondは41歳，母Dionese Bowermanは28歳であった。1552年2月5日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアでWinifred Leighと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。1587年，53歳の時，職業はlord mayor of londonと記録されている。彼は1592年3月4日にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで58歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.St Stephen Walbrook with St Benet Shereh: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 스티븐 월브룩 위스 스트 베넷 셔호그

ko.lifeSummary: Sir George Bond(이)가 1534년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 웨스트 벅랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Bond(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Dionese Bowerman(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1552년 2월 5일 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 Winifred Leigh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 1587년, 53세의 나이로 그의 직업은 lord mayor of london이었다. 그는 1592년 3월 4일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir George Bond nasceu em 1534, em West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Bond, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Dionese Bowerman, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Winifred Leigh em 5 de fevereiro de 1552, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 8 filhas. Em 1587, com 53 anos, sua profissão aparece como lord mayor of london. Ele faleceu em 4 de março de 1592, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir George Bond родился в 1534 г. в местности вест буккланд, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William Bond было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Dionese Bowerman, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Winifred Leigh 5 февраля 1552 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 8 дочерей. В 1587 году, в возрасте 53 лет,  он работал по профессии lord mayor of london. Он умер 4 марта 1592 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir George Bond nació en 1534, en West Buckland, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Bond, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Dionese Bowerman, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Winifred Leigh el 5 de febrero de 1552, en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 8 hijas. En 1587, a la edad de 53 años, su ocupación registrada era lord mayor of london. Murió el 4 de marzo de 1592, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
